title: Falafel Vegetarian Plate Tahini

seo.title: Vegetarian Falafel Recipe: Authentic Middle Eastern Plate
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making delicious, authentic Vegetarian Falafel at home! This Middle Eastern plate features crispy falafel, fresh veggies, and creamy tahini. Get the easy recipe now!
longTailKeywords: vegetarian falafel recipe step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Vegetarian Falafel Recipe with Creamy Tahini
h2Headings: What is Falafel and Why You'll Love It?
Ingredients You'll Need for Perfect Falafel
Pro Tips for the Best Results
Serving Suggestions for Your Falafel Plate
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant Middle Eastern vegetarian falafel plate with crispy falafel, fresh salad, pickles, and creamy tahini sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of the Middle East with this homemade vegetarian falafel plate, complete with a drizzle of rich tahini.

description: Savor the rich flavors of this authentic Middle Eastern Falafel Vegetarian Plate, a delightful meal perfect for any occasion. Crispy, golden-brown falafel patties are served alongside a fresh medley of vegetables, tangy pickles, and a generous drizzle of creamy tahini sauce. This homemade recipe brings the vibrant tastes of the Levant right to your kitchen table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chickpeas
instructions.text: Ensure your dried chickpeas have been soaked in plenty of cold water for at least 12-18 hours. Drain them completely and pat dry with a clean towel. They should still be firm, not soft like canned chickpeas.

instructions.title: Blend the Falafel Mixture
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the drained chickpeas, chopped onion, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, garlic cloves, ground cumin, ground coriander, baking soda, salt, and black pepper. Pulse until the mixture is finely ground but still has some texture, not a paste. Do not over-process.

instructions.title: Chill and Form Patties
instructions.text: Transfer the falafel mixture to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. This helps the mixture firm up. Using a falafel scoop or your hands, form the mixture into small, disc-shaped patties or balls, about 1.5 inches in diameter.

instructions.title: Prepare for Frying
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot or heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-high heat to about 350-375F (175-190C). Make sure there is enough oil to submerge the falafel completely.

instructions.title: Fry the Falafel
instructions.text: Carefully lower a few falafel patties into the hot oil, being careful not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 3-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Repeat with the remaining falafel.

instructions.title: Make the Tahini Sauce
instructions.text: While the falafel fries, whisk together the tahini, fresh lemon juice, cold water, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl until smooth and creamy. Add more water if needed to reach your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Warm the pita bread. Arrange the crispy falafel on a serving platter with chopped tomatoes, chopped cucumbers, sliced pickled turnips, and fresh mint leaves. Drizzle generously with the creamy tahini sauce. Serve immediately with warm pita bread for making sandwiches or as a mezze plate.

tips: For truly authentic falafel, use a specialized falafel scoop (kalaib falafel) to create perfectly round, uniform patties that cook evenly.
Always use dried chickpeas soaked overnight, not canned chickpeas, for the best texture and flavor in your falafel. Canned chickpeas will result in a mushy texture.
Falafel is best served immediately after frying while still hot and crispy. Leftover falafel can be reheated in an oven or air fryer to restore crispness, but will not be as good as fresh.
article: Falafel Vegetarian Plate Tahini is a beloved Middle Eastern Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
